title: The necessity of souls in Evas other possibilities split
display_title: The necessity of souls in Evas other possibilities split
wikitext:
[]: The necessity of souls in Evas/other possibilities [split]             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Apr 10, 2015 8:47 pm [Split from "Was Rei really wrong?" ] NemZ wrote: The souls part is speculation. They might be entirely artificial, a dummy plug in a dummy socket.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 10, 2015 8:49 pm NemZ wrote: The souls part is speculation. They might be entirely artificial, a dummy plug in a dummy socket.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Apr 10, 2015 9:04 pm Reichu wrote: Rather than introduce a totally new concept ("dummy socket"), it seems simpler to just interpret their suicides as, at least in part, serving to liberate their human souls for HIP.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Apr 10, 2015 10:11 pm It's not a new concept, really. It's just an elaboration on the dummy plug system with soulless but otherwise operable eva bodies much like the test chamber things in ep14, and explains why they don't do any atf mojo the entire fight with Asuka... because they can't. The impalement ritual seemed to somehow trigger the great screaming wave. Speculation beyond that seems pointless. Show me the little red dot leaving those evas and I'll reconsider, but until then it's pure fanwank.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Apr 10, 2015 10:14 pm I never said there are no souls within the MPEs, only that the only thing required for them to function is some kind of copy of Kaworu's soul, so there is no "resident" soul within them. Only the fake-Kaworu in the dummy plug. Said ersatz bishonen controls the MPE bodies much the way Kaworu plays with Kyoko's body. The end goal of the Eva program is unmanned drone units, as an alternative to fragile and unreliable child soldier pilots. Nerv was probably working on Rei-based Lilith clone drone Evas on paper while actually doing... something... and SEELE doing the same, concealing their ritual abominations behind an unmanned Eva unit program. Hence why Asuka's Eva is a "production model" yet it's clearly unique in its characteristics as the others are and the actual "Mass Production" units are radically different. I think the idea of Nerv replicating the Kyoko/Yui accidents to manufacture Evas is over the top grimderp, raises too many questions, and is not textually supported. The kids in Shinji's class are all pilot candidates because they're orphans, and they're orphans because they survived a disaster of biblical proportions that doomed civilization to a slow death, not because Nerv kidnapped their moms and shoved them into Evas.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Apr 10, 2015 10:55 pm Really liking Chuckman's thoughts on the MP Evas, can't help but agree.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Apr 10, 2015 11:01 pm Oh I think Nerv does indeed have moms on ice somewhere in the basement, but by the end Seele doesn't have any need for such measures for exactly the reason you describe, Chuck.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Apr 11, 2015 1:46 am NemZ wrote: Oh I think Nerv does indeed have moms on ice somewhere in the basement,

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Apr 11, 2015 2:54 am Chuckman wrote: This may sound odd coming from me but this is just fanwank to make the series darker for no real reason with nothing in the show to back it up. Yes I am aware one of the production crew said there's an mother in every Eva but that's not the show .

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Apr 11, 2015 3:01 am I agree with all of that, and certainly it's what everyone in power wanted to eventually happen, but still think they ALSO have moms on ice so that the kids in 2A can function as backup pilots the old fashioned way. Without that it makes no sense at all for Toji to even be considered.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Apr 11, 2015 4:20 am I like Chuckman's ideas about the MPEs. One of the things we need to remember is that Rei and Kaworu are basically the same when it comes to the method of form and possession of abilities (established in Episode 24). Rei can sync with Prototype Model Unit 00, which has no mother's soul in it. I would think the MPEs would be about the same, what with the reference of the name of one who is said to be very similar to Rei in the show. The only difference between the sync methods of Unit 00 and the MPEs implied is that one used a physical body as a vessel for a soul for the Eva to sync to (Rei), while the other used the Dummy System as a vessel for a synthesized soul (Kaworu's) for the Evas to sync to. As for the comment from the production staff (and the inevitable discussion of Auteur Theory vs DoTA), no one who's worked on Eva tend to make a habit of referencing either Unit 00 or the MPEs when they talk about "proper" Evangelion units. If "all Evas have a mother's soul" includes the MPEs, then it should also include Unit 00, which would seem to contradict Episode 14's insight on Shinji's experience with the unit.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Apr 11, 2015 4:28 am Bagheera wrote: after 2I, Adam blew up and his soul "flew off somewhere". After that Seele (somehow) retrieved the soul and stuck it in Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Apr 11, 2015 4:30 am pwhodges wrote: This, of course, contradicts the idea Reichu is exploring (if my recollection is not too confused) of 2I being a sort of contact experiment resulting in Kaworu right away. My as yet uncontinued fragment Adam awoke follows the CI approach.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Apr 11, 2015 4:39 am NemZ wrote: It's not a new concept, really. It's just an elaboration on the dummy plug system with soulless but otherwise operable eva bodies much like the test chamber things in ep14, and explains why they don't do any atf mojo the entire fight with Asuka... because they can't.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Apr 11, 2015 5:05 am ^ Oh, they have something there, alright. I just tend to think they operate like a better functioning Unit 00 with a Dummy Plug than they would a "proper" Eva unit. In fact, I tend to see Unit 00 as a prototype to the MPEs, while Unit 01 was a forerunner testing the mechanics to units 02 and 03.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Apr 11, 2015 5:16 am . . . but all of them require souls. I see nothing in the text to suggest otherwise.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Apr 11, 2015 5:16 am Bagheera wrote: It implies a morgue, man.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Apr 11, 2015 5:38 am ^ Fair and factual points. But the Dummy Plugs were outside of the Evas, meant to replicate a pilot. We're discussing what's inside the MPE and Proto Type 00 units that's meant to sync with the fake Dummy Plug pilots to begin with. (Or, at least that's what I'm interested in discovering.)

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Apr 11, 2015 5:41 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: ^ Fair and factual points. But the Dummy Plugs were outside of the Evas, meant to replicate a pilot. We're discussing what's inside the MPE and Proto Type 00 units that's meant to sync with the fake Dummy Plug pilots to begin with.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Apr 11, 2015 5:44 am ^ Yes, but who's soul? Episode 14 suggests that there's "another" "Rei" (or something very Rei-like) in Unit 00. Is that a soul? If so, whoes is it? Where did it come from? Why is it that both Rei and Shinji can sync with it, but Asuka can't? And is the same true for the MPEs?
